BOOSEapp Documentation
Table of Contents

Namespace BOOSEapp

Classes

AppArray

Declares an int or real array stored inside AppStoredProgram. Syntax: array int nums 10 array real prices 10

AppCall
AppCanvas

Custom canvas implementation for the BOOSE application that handles drawing operations on bitmaps.

AppCircle

Represents a command that draws a circle on the canvas with a specified radius.

AppCommandFactory

Factory class for custom application commands including Circle, Rect, and MoveTo.

AppElse

Represents the "else" branch of a conditional command within a program sequence.

AppEnd

Represents the "end" command that marks the end of a compound command block, such as "if", "while", or "for".

AppFor

Represents a "for" loop command that iterates a variable form a starting value to an end value with an optional step icrement. The command supports syntax such as "for i = 1 to 10 step 2", where "i" is the loop variable, "1" is the starting value, "10" is the ending value, and "2" is the step.

AppIf
AppInt

Unrestricted integer variable implementation Replaces the sealed BOOSE.Int class

AppMoveTo

Represents a command that moves the application canvas to the specified coordinates.

AppParser

Extends the base Parser to support expressions and the BOOSE language features used in the BOOSE app, such as method definitions and unrestricted variable assignment.

AppPeek

Represents the "peek" command that retrieves a value from an array at a specified index and assigns it to a variable.

AppPoke

Represents the "poke" command that assigns a value to an element of an array variable.

AppReal

Represnts an unrestricted real variable declaration and assignment command. Replaces the sealed BOOSE.Real class.

AppRect

Represents a command that draws a rectangle on the canvas with specified width and height.

AppStoredProgram

Extends the base StoredProgram class to add support for integer and real arrays, as well as tracking limits on boolean and compound commands.

AppWhile

Represents the "while" compound command, which executes a block of commands repeatedly as long as a specified condition is true.

AppWriteText

Represents the "write" commmand that renders text string on the canvas at the current cursor position. The command can take a string literal, a variable name, or an expression that evaluates to a string as its parameter.

Form1

The main form for the BOOSE application, providing UI and program execution logic.

Interfaces

IAppCanvas

Provides additional drawing methods for an application canvas, extending BOOSE.ICanvas.

Made with docfx
